dialogue.utterance: Unit 3 respond to Arthur Ashe Blvd for a motor vehicle collision with reported back and left arm pain in a 26-year-old.
Hi, I'm Alex from the rescue squad. Tell me what happened and what brought you to call 911?
I got into a car crash. I have lower back and left arm pain. It feels like an 8 right now.
Let's check your awareness. What is your name?
Amber.
What city are we in and when is your birthday?.
[CITY]. July 14th. 8. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.: signs_and_symptoms; EMT: Any other areas hurting, and do you remember the crash?.
Left hand pain too. Don't remember the whole thing - just exiting the car. 10. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.: medications; EMT: Any medications or known allergies you take regularly?.
No regular meds, but I'm allergic to grass pollen.
What did you eat or drink before the crash?
I had two or three beers at a work event. Nothing else since.
What's the last thing you remember before the crash?
I was driving home. Don't remember the crash, only getting out and being in pain.
Amber, how many fingers do you see?.
Three.
Good. You seem alert and oriented x4 with a GCS of 15.
On a 0 to 10 scale, how bad is your pain now? 20. Pain Assessment; O.P.Q.R.S.T.: severity; Patient: A 10, but it feels mostly in my arm and lower back now.
Let me check your airway. Can you speak and breathe deeply?.
Yes, I'm fine to talk and breathe.
Airway is patent with no signs of obstruction. No preferred positioning needed.
Breathing is spontaneous and unlabored. Let's get your vitals now.
Her pulse is 146, very tachycardic.
Respirations are normal at 18 per minute.
Blood pressure is 125 over 62, and it is stable.
Oxygen saturation is 99%, and her skin is pink and warm with normal turgor.
Blood sugar is at 100, within normal range.
No evidence of shock - heart rate is fast but skin and circulation look good for now.
Let's secure your spinal column with a cervical collar in case of possible injury.
Pupils are PEARL at 4 mm. No signs of neck tenderness or tracheal deviation. No JVD.
Head is normocephalic, and you are not reporting dizziness or visual issues.
Just pain in the back and arm, you know.
Skin is intact with normal color and turgor - only a 4-inch, 1-inch-deep laceration to the left arm.
Chest is symmetric and atraumatic. Breath sounds are clear and equal bilaterally.
You're able to move your arms and legs. Any back deformity or numbness?.
Just pain when I try to move.
EKG findings on monitor: Sinus tachycardia - heartbeat is normal but fast.
The 4-lead ECG gives a clear reading with no signs of other abnormalities.
Retaking vitals: Heart rate is now 130; BP is 94 over 74, and SpO2 is 96. Blood sugar reading is 0 due to monitor error.
Any change in your pain level over the last hour?.
Still around 10, but it's not radiating anymore, just stays in the arm.
Did you black out after the crash before exiting the vehicle?.
Yes, I lost consciousness for a brief moment. Woke up outside the car and in pain.
You'll be transported to [COMPANY_NAME]. That okay with you, Amber?.
Yeah, anywhere is fine as long as I get help.
We'll move you to the stretcher using the chair and harness system.
Be easy with me, my arm is sore.
I'll make sure it's as smooth as possible. Partner, ready the chair for lift.
We have her secured and ready for transfer to the stretcher.
Amber, how are you feeling on the chair now?.
Not too bad, better than I thought.
Partner, we need to place spinal immobilization before securing her to the stretcher.
C-collar and backboard applied, no movement noted during transfer.
She has a long laceration on the left arm and pain without signs of neurologic deficit.
[COMPANY_NAME], this is Unit 3. 26-year-old female from MVC with 10/10 pain-lower back, left arm, and forearm. IV in place. C-collar applied and baseline vitals transmitted.
No active bleeding. Pupils equal and reactive. Currently alert and oriented.
Spontaneous breathing. Neurology normal to our assessment. On the way now.
Transfer to the hospital bed will be done via sheet pull. No complications anticipated.
Protocol 4-1 for general trauma managed per field impression.
All steps completed. Let's proceed with the transfer.
Amber, how is the pain now that you're on the stretcher?.
Still 10. Nothing changed.
No issues noted with our patient en route. Would you like to record a quick update, Partner?.
All clear. No new signs or changes to patient condition.
Partner, prepare for hospital Bed contact in 10 seconds. I'll alert the hospital now.
[COMPANY_NAME], this is Unit 3. Arrival imminent with a 26-year-old female post MVC with lower back and left arm pain. Spinal immobilized with C-collar in place.
Patient alert, oriented, and stable with ongoing 10/10 pain. No new findings from last assessment.
911, from Unit 3. Amber transferred safely and conscious to receiving hospital. All facilities cleared.
